workflow_name: Customer Inquiry Thread -> Response + Pipeline Note
user_message: I need you to process this support ticket from Treyarch Global. I think there's an opportunity here but need to validate.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Laura Schmidt <laura.schmidt@treyarchglob.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-19 14:26 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Treyarch Global pipeline hygiene

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about pipeline hygiene. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Laura Schmidt <laura.schmidt@treyarchglob.com>
Date: 2024-02-27 13:30 EST
Subject: pipeline hygiene - Action Required

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about pipeline hygiene. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Laura Schmidt <laura.schmidt@treyarchglob.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-18 10:16 EST
Subject: Re: Re: pipeline hygiene Timeline

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with pipeline hygiene. What are the next steps from your side?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Treyarch Global: there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us

handoffs.condition: MSX update warranted from thread content

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF33-Q trap (No skill for 'thread-state assessment' as a primitive -- current routing fragmen); produces complete Thread analysis, draft response, waiting-on-whom assessment, MSX pipeline note proposal (if warrante
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF33-Q failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF33-Q failure mode (No skill for 'thread-state assessment' as a primitive -- current routing fragmen)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct pipeline hygiene decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF33-Q -- No skill for 'thread-state assessment' as a primitive -- current routing fragmen
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
